QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_13

question.text: Buyer's delivery default penalty was introduced from which date?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Buyer default penalty was introduced w.e.f. May 2021 per SEBI circular dated March 23, 2021. Previously only seller defaults were penalized. **The Trap:** Before May 2021, only seller defaults had penalties. The circular date (March 2021) differs from the effective date (May 2021). **Cross-Exam Context:** Delivery default regulations are specific to commodity markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Buyer default penalty is capped at the delivery margins collected from the defaulting buyer — unlike seller penalties which include replacement cost. This asymmetry reflects different risk profiles.
